summary: Russia has claimed control of Shevchenko in Kharkiv region and continues drone attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure, while Ukrainian forces report successful strikes on Russian logistics corridors using Hornet UAVs. Energy facilities in Sumy and other regions suffered Russian attacks, causing power outages. Both sides report ongoing aerial activity and civilian casualties.
key_points: Russia announced control of Shevchenko in Kharkiv oblast (Source: @tass_agency/379573).
Ukrainian 3rd OSPP reported Hornet UAV strikes disrupting the Melitopol‑Chongar supply route (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
Multiple Shahed‑136 drone launches reported from the Black Sea and Kursk/Orla areas targeting Odessa and other locations (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Russian attacks on Sumy region energy infrastructure caused power outages (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
A Russian drone strike injured a 10‑year‑old child in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/81738).
Four civilians, including a child, were injured by a drone attack in Leningrad Oblast (Source: @tass_agency/379546).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" observed over the Black Sea near Vylkove and other points in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).

sections.executive_summary: Russia claims control of Shevchenko in Kharkiv region, intensifying ground operations. Ukrainian Hornet UAVs have struck Russian logistics on the Melitopol‑Chongar corridor, disrupting supply lines. Both sides continue extensive drone activity, with civilian infrastructure and casualties increasingly affected.

points: The 3rd Operational‑Strategic Support and Protection (OSSP) unit reported Hornet strike UAVs destroying vehicles and logistics on the Melitopol‑Chongar route, complicating Russian fuel and supply deliveries to the peninsula (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
Ukrainian forces reported a Russian drone hit a car carrying Oleg Tyagnibok, leader of the Svoboda party, near Ivano‑Frankivsk; he survived with injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30723).

Ukrainian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" operated over the Black Sea near Vylkove and conducted surveillance over Stepanivka, Królevets, and other points in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).
Ukrainian authorities noted that Russian attacks on Sumy’s energy infrastructure caused power outages, with local utilities working to restore service (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
A Russian drone strike in Zaporizhzhia injured a 10‑year‑old child, wounding his hand, and also injured the child's father (Source: @UkraineNow/81738).
TASS reported that Russian forces have established control over the settlement of Shevchenko in Kharkiv oblast (Source: @tass_agency/379573).
Russian officials, including Foreign Minister Lavrov, reiterated the goal of restoring full rights for Russian‑speaking citizens in Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81736).
A Russian drone attack in Leningrad Oblast injured four civilians, including a child, according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/379546).
Russian media noted the development of the sixth version of the "Bulat" drone detector capable of streaming video from detected UAVs (Source: @tass_agency/379573).
Russian forces claimed a strike on a Ukrainian car carrying a political leader, confirming UAV activity in western Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30723).
Ukrainian reports indicate ongoing infantry battles in the Shostka district of Sumy, with Ukrainian paratroopers advancing up to 700 m in multiple sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned on the eastern direction, suggesting heightened air operations near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64220).
Ukrainian forces observed a Russian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" over the Black Sea near Vylkove, indicating surveillance of eastern maritime approaches (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).
Multiple Shahed‑136 drone launches from Kursk and Orla regions targeted Odessa and surrounding areas, affecting eastern coastal defenses (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Ukrainian OSPP Hornet UAV strikes disrupted Russian logistics on the Melitopol‑Chongar corridor, a key eastern supply line (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
A Russian drone attack in Leningrad Oblast injured four civilians, including a child, indicating northern aerial threats (Source: @tass_agency/379546).
Ukrainian intelligence reported a hostile reconnaissance UAV from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa, suggesting northern‑southern axis activity (Source: @kpszsu/64234).
Ukrainian sources noted a Russian drone strike on a car near Ivano‑Frankivsk, showing northern‑west UAV penetration (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30723).
TASS announced development of the "Bulat" drone detector, potentially enhancing Russian air defence in the north (Source: @tass_agency/379573).
Ukrainian reports of Shahed‑136 launches from Kursk and Orla regions could affect northern oblasts such as Bryansk (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Ukrainian reports of Shahed‑136 drone launches from the Black Sea targeting Odessa and the southern coast (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Russian attacks on energy infrastructure in Sumy (northern) and Zaporizhzhia (southern) caused civilian power disruptions (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
Ukrainian Hornet UAV strikes on the Melitopol‑Chongar route disrupted Russian logistics to the Crimean peninsula (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
A Russian drone strike injured a child in Zaporizhzhia, highlighting civilian risk on the southern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81738).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" operated over the Black Sea near Vylkove, a strategic point for southern maritime operations (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).
Multiple Shahed‑136 drone launches reported from the Black Sea, Kursk, and Orla regions, targeting Odessa and other locations (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Ukrainian Hornet UAVs successfully engaged Russian ground logistics on the Melitopol‑Chongar corridor (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
Russian tactical aviation activity warned on the eastern direction, indicating potential air strikes near front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/64220).

Russian drone attacks caused civilian injuries in Leningrad Oblast and Zaporizhzhia, showing continued UAV use against both military and civilian targets (Sources: @tass_agency/379546, @UkraineNow/81738).
TASS reported development of the sixth version of the "Bulat" drone detector, enhancing Russian capability to detect and stream video from hostile UAVs (Source: @tass_agency/379573).

key_developments: Russia announced control of Shevchenko in Kharkiv oblast, indicating a territorial gain on the front (Source: @tass_agency/379573).
Ukrainian Hornet UAVs struck Russian logistics on the Melitopol‑Chongar corridor, disrupting fuel and supply lines to Crimea (Source: @DeepStateUA/23553).
Shahed‑136 drones were launched from the Black Sea and Kursk/Orla areas, targeting Odessa and other southern locations (Source: @eRadarrua/95669).
Russian attacks on Sumy’s energy infrastructure caused power outages, affecting civilian populations (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
A Russian drone strike injured a 10‑year‑old child in Zaporizhzhia, highlighting civilian risk from aerial attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81738).
Four civilians, including a child, were injured by a drone attack in Leningrad Oblast (Source: @tass_agency/379546).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" conducted surveillance over the Black Sea near Vylkove and over Sumy and Zaporizhzhia districts (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).
sections.threat_assessment: Both sides are intensifying UAV operations, with Ukraine employing Hornet strike drones against Russian logistics and Russia deploying Shahed‑136 loitering munitions against Ukrainian infrastructure. Civilian areas are increasingly vulnerable, as evidenced by injuries in Zaporizhzhia and Leningrad Oblast. Control of key settlements like Shevchenko suggests ongoing ground offensives, while advanced Russian drone detection systems may mitigate some Ukrainian aerial threats.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue diplomatic engagement, with Germany’s Chancellor Merz indicating readiness for negotiations, while Ukraine seeks additional military aid, as reflected in local fundraising efforts for vehicle repairs. Russia emphasizes protection of Russian‑speaking populations, framing its actions as safeguarding rights, which may influence its operational posture in occupied territories.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed‑136 launch sites in Kursk, Orla, and the Black Sea is critical for early warning. Ukrainian Hornet UAV activity on the Melitopol‑Chongar route demonstrates effective interdiction of Russian supply lines and should be tracked for pattern analysis. Civilian casualty reports underscore the need for reinforced air defence in both southern and northern civilian zones.
